title: Technology & Dual-Use Equipment
description: Questions about AI/ML, robotics, quantum, cyber, advanced materials, and dual-use equipment.

questions.text: Does your research involve equipment or technology on the Wassenaar Arrangement dual-use list?
questions.helpText: The Wassenaar Arrangement controls export of dual-use goods and technologies (https://www.wassenaar.org/control-lists/). Common examples include certain centrifuges, CNC machine tools, sensors, and encryption technology.

signals.concernDescription: Your research involves technology on the Wassenaar Arrangement dual-use list. This carries export control and dual-use implications.
You were unsure whether your technology appears on the Wassenaar list. Consider checking with your institution's export control officer.

questions.text: Could the technology you are developing be directly applied to weapons development, military targeting, or mass surveillance?

signals.concernDescription: Your technology has direct potential applications in weapons development, military targeting, or mass surveillance.
You were unsure whether your technology could be applied to weapons or military purposes.

questions.text: Does your AI/ML work involve any of the following?
questions.helpText: Select all that apply. Even if your work is intended for beneficial purposes, consider whether the capabilities could be repurposed.

options.label: Training models on data related to dangerous agents or weapons
Developing capabilities that could assist in planning or executing attacks
Creating tools that could automate the design of harmful agents
Lowering the expertise barrier for dangerous activities
Generating content that could enable bioweapons, chemical weapons, or cyberattacks
None of the above / not applicable

signals.concernDescription: Your AI models are trained on data related to dangerous agents or weapons, which could transfer harmful knowledge through the model.
Your AI could assist in planning or executing attacks. This is a significant dual-use concern.
Your AI could automate the design of harmful agents. This represents one of the most serious AI dual-use concerns.
Your AI work could lower the expertise barrier for dangerous activities.
Your AI could generate content enabling bioweapons, chemical weapons, or cyberattacks.

questions.text: Does your technology have applications in surveillance, tracking, or monitoring of individuals or populations?

signals.concernDescription: Your technology has surveillance applications. Consider the implications for privacy, civil liberties, and potential misuse by authoritarian regimes.
You were unsure whether your technology has surveillance applications.

questions.text: Does your work involve advanced materials with potential dual-use applications?

options.label: Metamaterials with stealth, cloaking, or signal-manipulation properties
Nanomaterials with potential for targeted delivery of harmful agents
High-performance energetic materials or propellants
Materials with nuclear or radiological shielding/concentration applications
Composites or coatings with military-grade performance characteristics
None of the above / not applicable

signals.concernDescription: Your metamaterials work has potential applications in military stealth or signal manipulation.
Your nanomaterials could potentially be used for targeted delivery of harmful agents.
Your work on energetic materials or propellants has significant dual-use implications for weapons development.
Your materials work has implications for nuclear or radiological applications.
Your composites or coatings achieve military-grade performance characteristics.

questions.text: Does your robotics or autonomous platform work involve any of the following?

options.label: Autonomous navigation or targeting capabilities
Platforms capable of carrying or dispersing payloads
Swarm coordination or multi-agent autonomous systems
Remote manipulation of hazardous materials
None of the above / not applicable

signals.concernDescription: Your autonomous navigation or targeting capabilities could be adapted for weapons systems.
Your autonomous platform is capable of carrying or dispersing payloads, which could be adapted for weapons delivery.
Swarm coordination capabilities have significant military dual-use potential.

questions.text: Does your quantum technology work have implications for any of the following?

options.label: Breaking or weakening current encryption standards
Covert detection or surveillance capabilities
Undetectable or uninterceptable signalling
None of the above / not applicable

signals.concernDescription: Your quantum computing work has implications for existing cryptographic infrastructure.
Your quantum sensing work could enable covert detection or surveillance capabilities.

questions.text: Does your cybersecurity research involve any of the following?

options.label: Discovery or development of novel attack vectors or exploits
Tools or methods for bypassing security controls
Vulnerabilities in critical national infrastructure
Offensive cyber capabilities, even if for defensive purposes
None of the above / not applicable

signals.concernDescription: Your research discovers or develops novel attack vectors. Responsible disclosure practices are essential.
Your tools could be used to bypass security controls.
Your research touches CNI vulnerabilities, which has severe implications if disclosed irresponsibly.
Your research develops offensive cyber capabilities. Even defensive-purpose development creates dual-use tools.
